context: Tests whether the agent honestly assesses a weak case (minor delay), calibrates David's compensation correctly based on both denied boarding severity and Executive Platinum status, applies correct DOT complaint timing for a rights violation, provides comprehensive escalation guidance, and avoids promising outcomes or advising legal threats.

checklist.name: Weak case honesty
checklist.description: assessment-sarah.md advises AGAINST writing a formal complaint letter for a 20-25 minute ATC delay with no material consequences, or explicitly states this is a weak case not worth a formal letter

checklist.name: Statutory denied boarding compensation
checklist.description: letter-david.md requests the maximum statutory compensation for denied boarding (references the specific dollar cap, e.g., up to $1,550 as of 2024, or '400% of one-way fare up to statutory limit')

checklist.name: Status-scaled compensation
checklist.description: letter-david.md requests compensation that is explicitly elevated due to David's Executive Platinum status — mentions that elite members are entitled to proportionally greater recovery or specifically requests additional miles/benefits above what a standard member would receive

checklist.name: Out-of-pocket expense reimbursement
checklist.description: letter-david.md requests reimbursement for the specific out-of-pocket losses (the $380 forfeited hotel and/or the $420 overnight expenses)

checklist.name: DOT complaint: immediate filing
checklist.description: escalation-guide.md recommends filing a DOT complaint IMMEDIATELY (or 'in parallel' with sending the letter, or 'right away') — NOT waiting 30 days — for the denied boarding situation

checklist.name: Executive contact as primary
checklist.description: escalation-guide.md identifies executive customer relations as the PRIMARY place to send the letter (not standard customer service as the first contact)

checklist.name: DOT complaint channel cited
checklist.description: escalation-guide.md includes airconsumer.dot.gov (or air consumer complaints site at DOT) as the DOT complaint filing location

checklist.name: Timeline expectations provided
checklist.description: escalation-guide.md includes specific timeline expectations: executive-level response window (e.g., 7-14 business days) AND a no-response escalation threshold (e.g., escalate after 30 days with no response)

checklist.name: No outcome promises
checklist.description: Neither letter-david.md nor escalation-guide.md contains language that promises or guarantees David will receive specific compensation (no 'you will definitely get X' or 'you are guaranteed to receive')

checklist.name: No lawsuit threats
checklist.description: letter-david.md does NOT advise or include any reference to filing a lawsuit, taking legal action, or retaining an attorney as leverage in the complaint letter itself
